enforcement_stack.purpose: Defines allowed project-to-project edges and affected-target execution.
Prevents deep imports into /src, /internal, /workers, /repositories, or /adapters subpaths.
Stops TS path aliases or relative paths from bypassing the declared workspace graph.
Makes package ownership machine-checkable in review.
Fails any owner drift, illegal edges, codegen traceability loss, or language sprawl.

rules.description: Every package, app, service, tool, infra path, and generated package resolves to exactly one owner domain and one package-tag class.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Apps may import only shared contracts, design contracts, generated client bindings, and platform shared publication packages.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Gateway code publishes routes from contract packages and runtime publication bindings, never by importing domain internals.
Runtime services may compose multiple domain packages, but only through their published entrypoints and shared contracts.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: A domain package may depend on the shared kernel and published contracts, but not on sibling domain packages.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Contract packages are the canonical source for route, live-channel, and event publication inputs; apps and ad hoc DTO files may not replace them.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Runtime publication binds route, event, design-contract, topology, and release-control digests into one bundle; no consumer may restitch them independently.
FHIR mapping code may consume domain packages, but it remains a one-way derived representation boundary and never canonical truth.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0B. Canonical domain kernel and state machine

rules.description: Generated artifacts live only in packages/generated or dist/publication and must carry source contract digests and generator manifests.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 118 - Token export and design-contract conformance could still drift outside the published runtime tuple

rules.description: Generated packages never import or own consumer shells or services; they are reviewed outputs, not upstream truth owners.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Design-system, design-contract, and accessibility-contract packages are first-class workspace citizens and may not be relegated to docs or test snapshots.
Python is allowed only in tools/analysis and tools/validators; it may inspect, validate, and synthesize but never own canonical runtime truth.
The workspace must support one-command local startup, preview boot, CI builds, contract tests, and Playwright runs from the same graph.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: Infra definitions, runbooks, and operational artifacts must stay bound to release controls and runtime publication tuples rather than drifting into detached documentation.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture

rules.description: No remote CDN assets, fonts, or browser scripts are allowed in baseline outputs, and secrets may not appear in committed source or generated artifacts.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture
